Bead ddx-d57d98d7 — [artifact-run-arch] ship effort-estimate skill

STATUS: deliverable already shipped; no new commit produced this attempt.

What is done
------------
The skill file required by the bead is present in-tree at the base
revision of this attempt:

  Path:   skills/ddx/effort-estimate/SKILL.md
  Size:   ~7.2 KB / 221 lines
  Blob:   c9e8b088db2928576d26d69352ad36dcb7c15e5f
  Landed: commit 12ec2b32 "feat(skills): ship effort-estimate skill
          [ddx-d57d98d7]" (Wed Apr 29 19:00:38 2026 -0400)

`git ls-tree 9ddbc1cf skills/ddx/effort-estimate/SKILL.md` confirms the
blob is reachable from the current base revision (9ddbc1cf, this
attempt's `base-rev`). The plan reference
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md
lists `effort-estimate` in the skill library section (#26 in the bead
breakdown); the shipped SKILL.md covers the workflow described there:
single- and multi-harness dispatch via `ddx run` / `ddx agent run`,
structured complexity/time/risk output contract, divergence-aware
aggregation rules across harnesses, evidence attachment via
`ddx bead evidence add`, and an anti-patterns section.

What blocked a new commit
-------------------------
The prior review note records:

  "REVIEW:BLOCK — Diff contains only execution manifest/result
   metadata. No skill files at skills/ddx/effort-estimate/ are present
   in the changed-files list, so the bead's deliverable cannot be
   evaluated."

This is a diff-window scoping mismatch, not a missing deliverable. The
skill was committed in attempt 12ec2b32, which is an ancestor of this
attempt's base-rev 9ddbc1cf. The review appears to be inspecting the
diff produced *after* the pre-execute-bead checkpoint rather than the
cumulative repository state, so a deliverable that already merged
shows up as "not in diff" even though it is in-tree.

Re-creating the file in a fresh commit would either:
  (a) be a no-op if the content is identical (git would refuse), or
  (b) shadow / diverge from the canonical version already shipped,
      producing two histories for the same artifact.

Neither is correct. The contract — "skill exists at
skills/ddx/effort-estimate/" — is satisfied.

What a follow-up would need
---------------------------
If the review tooling needs to see the skill in this attempt's diff,
the fix is in the review pipeline, not in this bead:

  1. Have the reviewer compare against the bead's plan reference or
     against `main`, not against the per-attempt checkpoint.
  2. Or, before queuing a re-attempt, verify whether the deliverable
     already merged in a prior attempt and close the bead directly
     instead of re-queueing.

If a stakeholder genuinely wants the SKILL.md content revised, the
follow-up bead should specify the gap (e.g., "add section X",
"correct CLI flag Y") so a meaningful diff can be produced. As
currently written, the bead's contract has no remaining work.
